# YourGP@Denman — Full Site Content

> General practice in Denman Prospect, Canberra — Genuine Care, Clinical Excellence.

---

## Home

A very warm welcome to YourGP@Denman.

Our practice vision is 'Genuine Care, Clinical Excellence'.

The best medicine is provided in the context of a long-term relationship with your GP. This relationship allows your GP to grow in understanding of who you are, your medical history and what is important to you. In this setting your GP can best tailor treatment to you and your circumstances, caring for you as a whole person. You can grow in trust of your GP, in their knowledge and genuine care for you and your family. Many GPs have established relationships with patients that cover decades. Some have the privilege of caring for four generations of the same family. These are relationships your GP would like to build with you.

Your GP provides clinical excellence through:
- Staying up to date with advances in medicine
- Fully computerised records and other technologies to support comprehensive care
- Continuing practice quality improvements
- Teaching the next generation of doctors by participating in the training of medical students and new GPs

Most importantly, your GP wishes to take the time to listen and explain. Your GP aims for people to leave with questions answered and a better understanding of their own health.

YourGP@Denman is partnered with YourGP@Crace and YourGP@Lyneham.

### Opening Hours

**Denman**: Monday to Friday 8:00am–5:00pm

### Locations

- YourGP@Denman — Level 1, 1B Felstead Vista, Denman Prospect ACT 2611
- YourGP@Crace — 1/5 Baratta Street, Crace ACT 2911
- YourGP@Lyneham — 62 Brigalow St, Lyneham ACT 2602

Phone: (02) 6109 0000

---

## Doctors

### Dr Elizabeth Muller
**New patients welcome**
Previously practising as a GP in Wanniassa, ACT, Elizabeth has developed experience across a broad range of general practice services — including children's health, preventive care, chronic disease monitoring and women's health. Elizabeth studied medicine at Australian National University (ANU), graduating with distinction in 2016. Following graduation, she completed her internship and residency in Canberra and Goulburn. Outside of work, Elizabeth enjoys contributing to the health and well-being of her community through general practice. She values compassionate care and continuous learning — striving always to keep up-to-date with best practices so she can provide excellent patient care.
**Availability**: Denman — Monday, Tuesday, Friday

### Dr Michelle de Jesus
**New patients welcome**
Dr De Jesus graduated from the University of Western Australia in 2012. She has spent the last 12 years in hospital medicine gaining experience across a wide range of medical and surgical specialties at Royal Perth Hospital as well as at The Canberra Hospital and Calvary Bruce Public Hospital as a medical registrar. She enjoys all areas of medicine and has special interests in General Medicine and chronic disease management as well as Geriatric medicine.
**Availability**: Denman — Tuesday, Thursday

### Dr Adam Wiley
**New patients welcome**
Dr Adam is a Canberra local but undertook most of his medical degree rurally in Tamworth and Armidale. Returning to the ACT he has spent extra years rotating through various specialties of medicine such as obstetrics, intensive care and cardiology. With a passion for holistic health care, education and all areas of general practice he is actively welcoming new patients. Whilst not in the office, Adam can be found hiking, bouldering and diving — having completed a certificate in wilderness medicine in the process.
**Availability**: Denman

### Dr Elliot Missen
**New patients welcome**
Elliott completed his medical degree from Monash University in 2019 and began training as a GP registrar in February 2024. Elliott has gained substantial experience in various medical and surgical specialties across metropolitan and rural centres. He believes in a holistic approach to patient care and is passionate about all aspects of General Practice. His work in several surgical specialties, including paediatrics and orthopaedics, has given him a wealth of knowledge and expertise.
**Availability**: Denman — Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday

### Dr Tracy Fox
**New patients welcome** (for pregnancy, babies and lactation)
Dr Tracy Fox is accepting new patients for pregnancy, babies and lactation. Her main area of interest in general practice is working with kids and their families. Tracy worked at Westmead Children's Hospital before moving into general practice. She completed her Diploma of Child Health in 2010 and regularly keeps updated with Women and Child Health conferences. She has an interest in postnatal checks, childhood development checks, antenatal breast-feeding advice and lactation medicine. Tracy completed her medical degree with honours from the University of Sydney in 2007. Outside of work, Tracy has 4 kids and enjoys baking, photography and using her Cricut as a creative outlet.
**Availability**: Denman — Monday, Wednesday

### Dr Maria Iannelli
**New patients welcome**
Maria enjoys all aspects of general practice but has a particular interest in paediatrics and adolescent health, women's health, preventive health care, and lactation medicine. Maria is a born and raised Canberran, although it was at the University of Melbourne that she obtained her Doctor of Medicine. She returned to Canberra to complete her training, specifically in General Practice due to her passion for quality care and patient advocacy. Outside of medicine, she is married with young daughters. She enjoys collecting cookbooks, hanging out with the family and watching the latest series on Netflix.
**Availability**: Denman — Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday

### Dr Chi So — Skin Cancer
**Accepting new patients for skin checks**
Dr Chi So is a GP with extensive experience in skin cancer diagnosis and management. He believes in an empathetic and caring approach to his consultations and can perform full body skin checks, mole and spot checks, dermoscopy, skin cancer surveillance, excisions, biopsies, and minor procedures. Dr So completed the requirements for a Fellowship of the Royal College of General Practitioners in 2017. Due to his special interest in skin cancer, he went on to complete the Master of Medicine (Skin Cancer) and is an experienced dermoscopist (skin microscopy for diagnosis of skin cancer). His approach is to balance the need for effective skin cancer treatment with the cosmetic outcomes of such treatment. Dr So has completed the Master of Public Health and has been involved in NHMRC-funded research projects in the field of skin cancer medicine. He has published research papers in international journals exploring the epidemiology and public health aspects of skin cancer management in Australian primary care.
**Availability**: Denman — Monday

### Dr Kathleen O'Brien
**New patients welcome**
Dr Kathleen O'Brien grew up in Canberra and is a graduate of the ANU Medical School. She is very friendly and loves children, and holds a Diploma in Child Health (Sydney Children's Hospital Network/University of Sydney). She has interests in paediatrics, women's health, and preventive health. She chose General Practice because she wanted the opportunity of establishing long lasting relationships with patients to provide excellent continuity of care. Dr O'Brien says she enjoys working at YourGP because of the commitment to quality care and the supportive team environment from the other doctors, to the nursing and administrative staff. Outside of work she likes bushwalks, travelling, reading, music, and her burgeoning vegetable garden.
**Availability**: Denman — Tuesday, Thursday

### Dr Somyata Jhorar
Dr Somya enjoys all areas of general practice however has a special interest in women's health, adolescent health and disease prevention. She places importance on open communication and patient centered care. Dr Somya completed her medical degree at the University of Queensland and holds a Diploma in Women's health. She has worked in regional and metropolitan areas of Queensland. Having recently moved to Canberra to be closer to her husband and continue her general practice training, she is looking forward to exploring the local region.
**Availability**: Denman — Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday

### Dr James Manley
**Not accepting new patients**
Dr James Manley is a Canberra local and graduate of the ANU. His interests include pain management, paediatrics and chronic disease management. He enjoys seeing elderly patients. Dr Manley is passionate about ensuring patients have continuity of care across the lifespan, enjoys seeing family units, and is dedicated to providing holistic care. Outside of the practice, Dr Manley plays guitar, follows the English Premier League, and spends quality time with his family.
**Availability**: Denman — Wednesday, Thursday, Friday

### Prof Stephen Martin
**Not accepting new patients**
Prof Stephen Martin loves helping people of all ages with their health. He has a special interest in men's, paediatric and adolescent health and is committed to compassionate healthcare, where patients are listened to and respected. Originally from New Zealand, he trained as a doctor here in Canberra, graduating in 2013 and has also completed a Diploma in Child Health. He has a passion for medical education and enjoys teaching medical students and registrars. Steve, his wife and three children love living in Canberra and all it has to offer. He enjoys running, cycling, and practicing Aikido. To unwind he dabbles in music and photography. He is the Professor of General Practice at The Australian National University.
**Availability**: Denman — Thursday

### Dr Anna Colwell — Travel Medicine
**New patients for travel consultations only**
Anna completed Medicine at the University of Tasmania. Since then she has practised predominately in Canberra but also in rural, remote and overseas places. She has had further training and experience in public health, infectious diseases and travel medicine, including obtaining a Masters of Public Health and Tropical Medicine, and Fellowship with the Australasian College of Tropical Medicine. Anna has worked for the Commonwealth Government in relation to immunisation, outbreaks, disaster planning and veteran's health and was a member of the ACT Health COVID Response Team. She has interests in lifestyle medicine, infectious diseases and travel health. Outside of work Anna enjoys hikes and rides with family and friends, reading, growing vegetables and creating cakes when there is a special event.
**Availability**: Denman — Wednesday

### Dr Mel Deery
**Not accepting new patients**
Mel enjoys all areas of general practice with special interests in paediatrics, women's health, pregnancy care and mental health. Mel loves being a GP and a practice owner. Along with her husband John, she is passionate about developing YourGP to better fulfil the vision of 'genuine care, clinical excellence'. She feels privileged to work alongside such an amazing team of doctors, nurses and administrative staff. She enjoys walking in Canberra, especially where there are views of the beautiful Brindabella mountain ranges. Currently available at YourGP@Denman every Friday and every second Monday. Mel is a graduate of the University of New South Wales 2000. Mel and John have three gorgeous children. They have a strong Christian faith.
**Availability**: Denman — Monday, Friday

### Dr John Deery — Vasectomy
**Accepting new vasectomy patients only**
John is providing pre vasectomy appointments for patients on Monday afternoons. From the moment John started General Practice in 2003, he desired to one day open an outstanding practice. He is passionate about providing amazing health care to patients. John loves sport and was previously a school Australian decathlon champion. He played for the St George Dragons, the Sydney City Roosters and Sydney University Rugby while studying. John is a devoted IT geek and keeps the practice up to date with technology. John graduated from the University of NSW in 2000. John and Mel have three gorgeous children. They have a strong Christian faith.
**Availability**: Denman — Monday; Lyneham — Wednesday; Crace — Thursday, Friday

### Dr Penny Hall
**Not accepting new patients**
**Availability**: Denman — Monday

---

## Services

### Children's Health
Paediatrics is a special interest of the GPs. Kids love to visit. The GPs and nurses all enjoy seeing children.

### Childhood Immunisations
GPs and nurses are supportive of childhood vaccinations. They are safe and an effective way to protect your child. Immunisation records are uploaded electronically to the Australian Immunisation Register.

### Chronic Disease Management
GPs are highly experienced at helping patients with:
1. Cardiovascular disease including myocardial infarction (heart attack) and post-MI care; valvular heart disease; hypertension (high blood pressure); heart failure.
2. Type 2 Diabetes both new-onset and established.
3. Osteoarthritis.
4. Chronic Kidney disease Stages 1 through 4.
5. Autoimmune diseases including rheumatoid arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, and psoriatic arthritis.

### Driver's Medicals
Commercial driving medical assessments require a double length appointment. For non-commercial licences for chronic medical conditions book a standard appointment.

### Men's Health
Check-ups, erectile dysfunction, incontinence, vasectomy, mental health and STD screens.

### Mental Health
Depression, anxiety, postnatal depression, schizophrenia, grief, bipolar depression and PTSD. A mental health care plan (referral to a psychologist) requires a double appointment.

### Minor Procedures
Implanon insertion and removal, IUD insertion and removal, skin cancer removal, ingrown toenail repair, tongue tie, vasectomy. Not all procedures are done by all doctors — please call the practice.

### IUD Insertion
Dr Roslyn Mackenzie at YourGP@Crace. This involves a visit to discuss IUD suitability and then a separate appointment for insertion.

### Musculoskeletal
Diagnosis and assessment of all types of joint and muscle sprains and strains.

### Tongue Tie
Dr John Deery only. Tongue tie is a common condition of newborn children. It will sometimes cause newborns to have a lot of difficulty with breastfeeding. He can provide prompt assessment and treatment of the condition, on the same day, when appropriate. Lip tie concerns are common and will rarely need surgical correction. If concerned please book for assessment.

### Travel Vaccinations
Yellow fever vaccine is available at Denman with Dr Anna Colwell or at Crace with Dr Krystel Chin and Dr Penny Gosling. Please bring any previous immunisation records. For a list of likely travel requirements please look up http://wwwnc.cdc.gov/travel/.

### Skin Checks
Dr Chi So is a GP with a special interest in skin cancer. Please do not wear make-up. At Crace, GPs Dr Ian Chan, Dr Julie Beuth, Dr Neena Sood and Dr Kit Wong have undertaken further skin cancer training. These doctors attract a higher fee — please see the billing section under FAQ.

### Vasectomy
Initial consult with Dr John Deery at Denman (Monday) or Lyneham (Wednesday). The procedure is performed at Crace on Thursday and Friday mornings. See canberravasectomy.com.au for more information.

### Women's Health
Check-ups, pap smears, STI screens, breast check, contraceptive advice, implanon insertion and removal, IUD insertion, menopause, pregnancy planning, shared antenatal care and postnatal check-ups.

### Workers Compensation
Payment is required at the time of your appointment. Standard consultation charges apply. Your insurer will reimburse you.

### After Hours
After Hours services are contained on the new patient page. Please navigate to https://www.yourgpdenman.com.au/patient/ for details.

### Allied Health

**Nursing**: Immunisations, ear syringe, wound management, health assessments, ECG, iron infusions, diabetes clinics, health assessments and chronic disease care plans. Bookings are not available online — please call for appointments.

**Pathology**: 4Cyte Pathology is available on-site, drop in without an appointment. They bulk bill, except for pathology that does not have a Medicare rebate. You will be advised of any out of pocket fees. Opening hours 8am to 2pm, Monday to Friday. Located behind reception.

---

## New Patient

### Your First Visit at YourGP@Denman

Save time waiting in the practice by completing your new patient registration online via HotDoc.

Please arrive at least ten minutes early to your first appointment. This will give you sufficient time to fill out forms and for reception to create your new patient file.

### Billing and Appointments

Payment is required at time of consultation. The GPs do not bulk bill.

All Workers Compensation cases are required to pay at time of consultation. Third Party (case conference) consultations will be billed to the insurer if there is an accepted claim number.

If a procedure or any other type of appointment is made then fees will be advised prior.

YourGP@Crace is open most Saturday mornings from 9am to 1pm. Saturday appointments attract higher fees. Please book online at yourgpcrace.com.au or call 6109 0000.

GPs reserve some appointments daily for urgent bookings. You can secure an 'on the day' appointment by booking online from midnight or by calling from 8:00am.

If you wish to cancel your appointment please give as much notice as possible. Missed appointments will attract a $50 fee. There is a cancellation list which you can place yourself on by booking online or by calling reception.

### Fees

- Level B Consultation (<20min): $117 ($107 for pensioners)
- Level C Consultation (<40min): $213 ($193 for pensioners)
- Level D Consultation (<60min): $317 ($281 for pensioners)
- Level E Consultation (>60min): $459 ($439 for pensioners)

### Saturday Fees (at YourGP@Crace)

- Level B Consultation (<20min): $146
- Level C Consultation (<40min): $240
- Level D Consultation (<60min): $364
- Level E Consultation (>60min): $488

### After Hours

Practice opening hours are 8am to 5pm Monday to Friday. A Saturday morning Crace clinic opens from 9am to 1pm.

For attention outside these hours please call CALMS on 1300 422 567.

CALMS (Canberra Afterhours Locum Service) is staffed by experienced Canberra GPs. It is a not-for-profit organisation that has been operating since 1971.

When you call you will speak with a nurse who will triage your medical problem. They may advise that it would be acceptable to wait and see your regular GP the following day.

If they advise you to see your GP the following day please access one of our emergency on the day bookings. These appointments become available online after midnight or by calling from 7.45am.

CALMS is only open after hours:
- 6pm–8:30am Monday to Friday
- 24 hours on weekends and public holidays

CALMS clinics operate until 11pm at Calvary Hospital Bruce and The Canberra Hospital. After 11pm a doctor is available to visit at home if you need urgent attention.

CALMS send the practice consultation notes the following morning so your GP is informed of your care.

CALMS charge a fee which will be advised on booking. If you have a health care card or concession card the fees are reduced.

For more information: calms.net.au

---

## FAQ

### Making Appointments
It is easy to book online. If you wish to call, please advise which practice you would like to visit and your preferred GP. You will be booked with your regular GP where possible. If your usual GP is not available, you are welcome to see another GP at Crace, Denman or Lyneham. They will have full access to your computer record. Your usual GP will be able to view that consultation at your next visit. If you think your appointment will require longer than fifteen minutes, please book a long appointment. If you wish to book a skin check or a mental health care plan, please advise reception so they can make the appropriate length appointment. If you wish to cancel your appointment, please give as much notice as possible. Missed appointments without notice will attract a $50 fee.

### Urgent Bookings
A proportion of appointments are reserved for urgent bookings each day. You can secure an 'on the day' appointment by booking online after midnight or by calling from 8am. If fully booked, we will try to find an appointment for you if your need is urgent. When fully booked, you can request a fit-in appointment. You can book on the fit-in list online. We will try our best to help you.

### Billing
Payment is required at the time of consultation. The GPs do not bulk bill. Consultation fees: Level B (<20min): $117 ($107 for pensioners). Level C (<40min): $213 ($193 for pensioners). Level D (<60min): $317 ($281 for pensioners). Level E (>60min): $459 ($439 for pensioners). Saturday fees: Level B: $146 | Level C: $240 | Level D: $364 | Level E: $488. If a procedure or any other appointment is made, fees will be advised beforehand. All Workers' Compensation/Third Party cases are required to pay at the consultation time. Missed appointments will attract a $50 fee. Further appointments will not be able to be made until the missed appointment fee is paid in full.

### Parking
There is easy and plentiful parking right outside the practice, including disabled parking.

### Languages Spoken
English. A phone interpreter can be provided if necessary. Please advise the language spoken and preferably give a few days notice to organise an interpreter. For deaf patients, the National Relay Service is available.

### Confidentiality
Your privacy is very important to us. Patient information is kept confidential. You may request the full privacy policy from reception or access it at the bottom of the webpage.

### Medical Certificates
A medical certificate is a legal document. Certificates cannot be backdated. Certificates will not be issued unless the GP is consulted.

### Results
The majority of results are notified via SMS. Any urgent results will be notified via phone call. Your results will be available online to view through your MyHealthRecord or MyDHR. ACT Pathology results are available to view immediately through MyDHR or the MyHealthRecord. Capital Pathology and Laverty Pathology results are available to view through your MyHealthRecord 7 days after the results have been processed.

### Contacting Your Doctor
Your GP can be contacted during business hours. Your GP will only take urgent calls at the time. Non-urgent calls will be taken as a message and your call will be returned. Please where possible let reception know what your call is regarding.

### Repeat Prescriptions
We request that you attend for review for a repeat prescription. This is best practice medicine. At your doctor's discretion a repeat prescription may be done without an appointment. This will be done in exceptional circumstances. An administration fee of $30 will apply. Please allow four working days.

### E-Mail
E-mail to reception is not secure. Please take care in disclosing confidential information. If you would like to forward a document to your doctor, please send to noreply@yourgpcanberra.com.au. Clinical advice cannot be given via email.

### Reminders
Your GP values preventative health care. You will be notified via SMS of reminders when due.

### Afterhours Care
Canberra Afterhours Locum Service (CALMS), please call 1300 422 567. CALMS provide us a summary of your visit.

### Accreditation
Accredited with AGPAL (Australian General Practice Accreditation Limited).

### Teaching Practice
YourGP is a teaching practice. Third year medical students from the ANU sit in with our GPs. We host one student in the practice for six week rotations, six times each year. Medical students appreciate participating in your care. We consider patients an important part of the teaching team. If your GP has a medical student you will be advised. You may decline to have a medical student present during your consultation. We also teach and provide supervision to GP registrars. A registrar is a fully qualified doctor who is undertaking further training in General Practice. Registrars have several years postgraduate experience in the hospital system. General practice training involves two years of work under a GP supervisor. Registrars are required to sit the Royal Australian College of General Practitioners fellowship exams.

### Suggestions or Complaints
Suggestions about how to improve our service are appreciated. The best way to provide feedback is via the 'contact us' form on the contact page. There is a section for feedback. If we cannot resolve your complaint, you may make an official complaint to the Community & Healthcare Complaints Commissioner, GPO Box 321, Canberra ACT 2601.

### Privacy Policy
Your privacy is important to us. Our full privacy policy (reviewed February 2026) is available on the Privacy Policy page, or you may request a copy from reception.

---

## Careers

### Join One of Canberra's Leading GP Practices

We're actively recruiting excellent GPs, nurses, and admin team members.

We care deeply about the people we serve. We pursue excellence every day. We continually seek to improve. We want to be the best.

3 Clinics | 33 Consulting Rooms | 30,000+ Patients per Year

### Why GPs Choose YourGP

We exist to serve GPs — so you can focus on what matters: your patients.

**Designed for Doctors**
- All consulting rooms at Crace have views to the outside — clinics designed so doctors actually want to work in them
- Surgical theatre space and nurse treatment rooms across all sites
- AI transcription tools available — camera and microphone in every room

**Clinical Excellence & Mentorship**
- Strong skin cancer, minor surgery, and joint injection team with mentorship available
- Develop your special interests — your patient panel reflects how you practise
- Preferred training location for GP registrars in Canberra

**Your Practice, Your Way**
- GPs are tenants hiring us to provide services and facilities so they can serve their patients
- Complete freedom — no lock-in contracts, no set hours, no pressure
- Excellent admin and nursing support so you can focus on patients
- You have full control over your hours and leave

**Strong Financials**
- 1.5% DNA rate — your time is respected
- Competitive service fee with strong billing discipline
- GP satisfaction surveyed every four months — we measure what matters

Full financial details in our GP Information Pack — express your interest to receive it.

**DPA Locations Available**
- Two of our three clinics (Crace and Denman) are in a Distribution Priority Area
- Visa sponsorship available for eligible international medical graduates

### What Your Day Looks Like as a GP

When you first join, we start you with a reduced patient load — as few as 2-3 patients per hour — so you have time to learn our systems and settle in. We then gradually increase your patient flow as you're ready. Our established GPs typically see 3-4 patients per hour.

Your patients already trust our brand, so about 80% of the trust gap is covered before you meet them. We'll help you create an introduction video so patients can see your personality before they book — this builds rapport before the first consultation.

We provide full administrative and nursing support. Our admin team handles billing, bookings, and correspondence. Our nursing team provides clinical support, patient preparation, and health coaching. You focus on medicine.

We do not currently have capacity for Level 1 Supervision for overseas trained doctors requiring supervision.

### Nurses

Are you a fantastic nurse who loves helping people and is always looking to improve? We might be the right place for you. Our nursing team are integral to the care we provide to patients and GPs. You'll have varied days with a lot of face-to-face patient time, health coaching, and clinical support. We invest in our team — minimum 20 hours of professional development per year, cross-training opportunities, and a supportive culture where you're valued. To thrive here, you enjoy helping others with care and excellence, and you're always looking to improve.

### Admin Team

Our GPs see over 30,000 patients a year across three clinics. A lot of support is required for the clinical team to deliver this level of care — and that's where you come in. You'll be the first point of contact for patients, the backbone of GP support, and a key part of how we deliver an excellent experience every day. Qualities that will see you thrive in this role: 1. You like learning and doing new things. 2. You get energised by helping others. 3. You have excellent attention to detail. We invest in our people — training, career pathways, and a workplace where you're supported to do your best work.

### Interested?

Whether you're a GP, nurse, or admin professional — we'd love to hear from you. Express your interest at: https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=IKXQ2SNEa0yqgAESdXTJaGhYc1dA2aRNq_7tqUENCfBURjNPQjk3MldQNDBGWlVUWDhHUENSOUZONy4u

---

## Privacy Policy

### 1. Introduction
YourGP is committed to protecting the privacy of your personal information and health information. This privacy policy explains how we manage your personal information and complies with the requirements of the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth), the Australian Privacy Principles (APPs), and the Health Records (Privacy and Access) Act 1997 (ACT). This policy was reviewed in February 2026.

### 2. Why and When Your Consent Is Necessary
When you register as a patient of our practice, you provide consent for our GPs and practice staff to access and use your personal information so they can provide you with the best possible healthcare. Only staff who need to see your personal information will have access to it. If we need to use your information for anything other than the primary purpose for which it was collected, we will seek additional consent from you, unless the use or disclosure is otherwise permitted or required by law.

### 3. Why Do We Collect, Use, Hold and Share Your Personal Information?
We collect your personal information to provide you with the highest level of healthcare. We may also use your information to:
- Manage your ongoing healthcare and communicate with other healthcare providers involved in your care
- Remind you of upcoming appointments or follow-up care
- Process claims through Medicare, the Department of Veterans' Affairs (DVA), or your private health fund
- Comply with our legal obligations, including mandatory reporting requirements
- Improve our services through clinical audits and quality assurance activities

### 4. What Personal Information Do We Collect?
The information we collect may include, but is not limited to:
- Names, date of birth, addresses, and contact details
- Medical information including medical history, medications, allergies, adverse events, immunisations, social history, family history, and risk factors
- Medicare number, DVA number, and private health fund details for claiming and identification purposes
- Healthcare identifiers as required under the Healthcare Identifiers Act 2010
- Clinical photographs where relevant to your care, with your informed consent

### 5. Dealing With Us Anonymously
You have the right to deal with us anonymously or under a pseudonym unless it is impracticable for us to do so, or unless we are required or authorised by law to deal with identified individuals. In most cases, to provide you with appropriate healthcare, we will need to know who you are.

### 6. How Is Personal Information Collected?
We collect information in several ways, including:
- When you complete a registration form at the practice (either paper-based or electronic)
- During the course of providing medical services, through consultations and examinations
- Via our website, email, SMS, or other electronic communications
- From your authorised guardian or responsible person if you are unable to provide information yourself
- From other healthcare providers such as specialists, hospitals, allied health professionals, and pathology or imaging services
- From your private health fund, Medicare, or DVA
- Through electronic prescribing, My Health Record, and online appointment booking services
- Through CCTV surveillance in common areas of the practice for security purposes

### 7. When, Why and With Whom Do We Share Your Personal Information?
We may share your personal information with third parties in the following circumstances:
- With other healthcare providers involved in your care, such as specialists, allied health professionals, hospitals, pathology and imaging services
- With Medicare, DVA, or your private health fund for claiming and billing purposes
- When required or authorised by law, including mandatory reporting obligations
- When necessary to lessen or prevent a serious threat to a patient's life, health, or safety, or to public health or safety
- In the case of a missing person, to assist in locating them
- For the establishment, exercise, or defence of an equitable claim

Some of our administrative and support functions are performed by staff based in Malaysia. These staff may access limited patient information (such as appointment details or billing information) as required to perform their duties. All such staff are bound by strict confidentiality agreements and operate under the same privacy obligations as our Australian-based staff. Other than the above, we will not share your personal information with overseas recipients without your express consent.

### 8. Will Your Information Be Used for Marketing Purposes?
We will not use your personal information for marketing purposes without your express consent. If you do provide consent, you may opt out of receiving marketing communications at any time by notifying us in writing.

### 9. How Are Artificial Intelligence (AI) Scribes Used?
Our practice may use AI-powered clinical scribes to assist doctors with documentation during consultations. The following safeguards are in place:
- Your informed consent is obtained at the beginning of each consultation before any AI scribe is activated
- Audio recordings used by the AI scribe are destroyed immediately after the clinical note is generated
- No patient data is stored or processed outside Australia
- All personally identifiable information (PII) is removed from any data used for AI model improvement

### 10. How Do We Store and Protect Your Personal Information?
We store your personal information in a combination of electronic and paper-based records. We take reasonable steps to protect your information from misuse, interference, loss, unauthorised access, modification, or disclosure. Our security measures include:
- Electronic records stored in secure, access-controlled clinical software systems
- Paper records stored in locked filing cabinets within secure areas of the practice
- Secure IT infrastructure with firewalls, encryption, and regular security updates
- Confidentiality agreements for all staff and contractors who access patient information

### 11. How Can You Access and Correct Your Personal Information Held by Us?
You have the right to request access to, or correction of, your personal information held by us. To do so, please submit a written request to our practice. We will respond to your request within 30 days. An administration fee may apply for the provision of records. In some circumstances, we may refuse access or correction in accordance with the Privacy Act 1988. If we refuse, we will provide you with written reasons and advise you of your right to complain.

### 12. How Can You Lodge a Privacy-Related Complaint?
If you believe your privacy has been breached or you wish to lodge a complaint, please contact us:
- Email: admin@ygp.au
- Post: YourGP@Crace, 1/5 Baratta Street, Crace ACT 2911
- Post: YourGP@Lyneham, 62 Brigalow St, Lyneham ACT 2602
- Post: YourGP@Denman, 1B Felstead Vista, Denman Prospect ACT 2611
- Phone: 02 6109 0000

We will investigate your complaint and respond within 30 days. If you are not satisfied with our response, you may lodge a complaint with the Office of the Australian Information Commissioner (OAIC):
- Phone: 1300 363 992
- Website: www.oaic.gov.au

### 13. Policy Review Statement
This privacy policy is reviewed regularly and updated as required. Any changes will be published on our website. In some cases, we may also communicate changes to you directly.
